title: Initialize Rust project structure
body:
## Goal
Create the foundational Rust project structure for rust-bucket.

## Tasks
1. Create `Cargo.toml` with:
   - name = "rust-bucket"
   - version = "0.1.0"
   - edition = "2024"
   - Required dependencies:
     - clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
     - serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
     - toml = "0.8"
     - rust-embed = "8"
     - thiserror = "2"
     - tempfile = "3"

2. Create `src/main.rs` with:
   - `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at the top
   - Minimal main() that just prints version for now
   - `mod lib;` or use the library crate

3. Create `src/lib.rs` with:
   - `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at the top
   - Module declarations (as stubs/comments for now):
     - `pub mod cli;`
     - `pub mod config;`
     - `pub mod generator;`
     - `pub mod templates;`
     - `pub mod verify;`

4. Create stub module files:
   - `src/cli.rs` (empty, just a comment)
   - `src/config.rs` (empty, just a comment)
   - `src/generator.rs` (empty, just a comment)
   - `src/templates.rs` (empty, just a comment)
   - `src/verify.rs` (empty, just a comment)

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo fmt --check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes (no warnings)
- All files have `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` where applicable

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for component overview
- Read STYLE_GUIDE.md for coding conventions

title: Create templates directory structure
body:
## Goal
Create the templates/ directory structure that will be embedded into the rust-bucket binary.

## Tasks
1. Create `templates/` directory at repo root

2. Create subdirectories matching the output structure:
   - `templates/.beads/` (for beads config)
   - `templates/.config/` (for nextest.toml)
   - `templates/.devcontainer/` (for devcontainer files)

3. Create a `templates/cargo-generate.toml` manifest file:
   ```toml
   [template]
   cargo_generate_version = ">=0.18.0"
   
   [placeholders]
   test_timeout = { type = "string", prompt = "Test timeout in seconds", default = "120" }
   rust_bucket_version = { type = "string", prompt = "rust-bucket version" }
   ```

4. Create placeholder files (empty or minimal) in each directory to establish structure:
   - `templates/AGENTS.md.liquid`
   - `templates/STYLE_GUIDE.md.liquid`
   - `templates/WORKFLOW.md.liquid`
   - `templates/TESTING.md.liquid`
   - `templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid`
   - `templates/deny.toml.liquid`
   - `templates/rustfmt.toml.liquid`
   - `templates/.devcontainer/Dockerfile.liquid`
   - `templates/.devcontainer/devcontainer.json.liquid`
   - `templates/.beads/config.yaml.liquid`

## Success Criteria
- Directory structure exists
- All placeholder files exist (can be empty)
- cargo-generate.toml is valid TOML

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md section on Template Pack
- Existing managed file list in README.md

title: Create AGENTS.md template
body:
## Goal
Convert the existing AGENTS.md into a Liquid template.

## Tasks
1. Read the current `/code/AGENTS.md` file

2. Create `templates/AGENTS.md.liquid` based on it:
   - Add version stamp at top: `<!-- Generated by rust-bucket v{{ rust_bucket_version }}. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. -->`
   - Keep all existing content
   - No other template variables needed for v1

3. The template should render to nearly identical content as the original

## Success Criteria
- `templates/AGENTS.md.liquid` exists
- Has version stamp comment at top
- Content matches original AGENTS.md (minus the hardcoded version)

## References
- Read existing AGENTS.md for content
- ARCHITECTURE.md for version stamping requirement

title: Create TESTING.md template
body:
## Goal
Convert the existing TESTING.md into a Liquid template with the test timeout as a variable.

## Tasks
1. Read the current `/code/TESTING.md` file

2. Create `templates/TESTING.md.liquid` based on it:
   - Add version stamp at top: `<!-- Generated by rust-bucket v{{ rust_bucket_version }}. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. -->`
   - Replace hardcoded timeout values (120 seconds) with `{{ test_timeout }}` template variable
   - Keep all other content

3. Specifically, template these lines:
   - "Every full test run must complete within {{ test_timeout }} seconds"
   - `profile.default.global-timeout = "{{ test_timeout }}s"`

## Success Criteria
- `templates/TESTING.md.liquid` exists
- Has version stamp comment at top
- Timeout values use `{{ test_timeout }}` variable
- Other content matches original TESTING.md

## References
- Read existing TESTING.md for content
- This is the ONLY template with a user-configurable variable in v1

title: Create .config/nextest.toml template
body:
## Goal
Create the nextest.toml template for cargo-nextest configuration.

## Tasks
1. Create `templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid` with:
   - Version stamp comment at top: `# Generated by rust-bucket v{{ rust_bucket_version }}. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.`
   - Global timeout setting using template variable:
     ```toml
     [profile.default]
     global-timeout = "{{ test_timeout }}s"
     ```

2. This file configures cargo-nextest per TESTING.md requirements

## Success Criteria
- `templates/.config/nextest.toml.liquid` exists
- Has version stamp comment
- Uses `{{ test_timeout }}` for the global timeout
- Valid TOML when rendered

## References
- TESTING.md for timeout requirements
- cargo-nextest documentation for config format

title: Create deny.toml template
body:
## Goal
Create the cargo-deny configuration template.

## Tasks
1. Create `templates/deny.toml.liquid` with:
   - Version stamp comment at top: `# Generated by rust-bucket v{{ rust_bucket_version }}. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.`
   - Reasonable default cargo-deny configuration:
     - Advisories check enabled
     - License check with common permissive licenses allowed (MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-*)
     - Bans section (empty but present)
     - Sources section (crates.io only)

2. This is a static template for v1 (no user-configurable variables)

## Success Criteria
- `templates/deny.toml.liquid` exists
- Has version stamp comment
- Valid TOML when rendered
- Sensible default deny configuration

## References
- cargo-deny documentation: https://embarkstudios.github.io/cargo-deny/

title: Implement config module
body:
## Goal
Implement the config module for loading/saving rust-bucket.toml.

## Tasks
1. Implement `src/config.rs` with:

2. Define the `Config` struct:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   pub struct Config {
       /// Version of rust-bucket that generated this config
       pub rust_bucket_version: String,
       /// Test timeout in seconds (default: 120)
       pub test_timeout: u32,
   }
   ```

3. Implement `Config::default()` with:
   - rust_bucket_version from env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
   - test_timeout = 120

4. Implement loading:
   ```rust
   pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Config, ConfigError>
   ```

5. Implement saving:
   ```rust
   pub fn save(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<(), ConfigError>
   ```

6. Define `ConfigError` enum using thiserror:
   - IoError
   - ParseError
   - ValidationError

7. The saved file should include a comment header:
   ```toml
   # Generated by rust-bucket vX.Y.Z. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.
   ```

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Unit tests for load/save round-trip
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for config requirements
- Read STYLE_GUIDE.md for error handling rules

title: Implement CLI module
body:
## Goal
Implement the CLI module using clap for argument parsing.

## Tasks
1. Implement `src/cli.rs` with clap derive macros

2. Define the CLI structure:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Parser)]
   #[command(name = "rust-bucket")]
   #[command(about = "Rust-first project bootstrapper for AI-first engineering")]
   pub struct Cli {
       #[command(subcommand)]
       pub command: Commands,
   }
   
   #[derive(Subcommand)]
   pub enum Commands {
       /// Apply rust-bucket to the current directory
       Apply {
           /// Force overwrite of existing managed files
           #[arg(long)]
           force: bool,
       },
   }
   ```

3. Implement interactive prompting:
   ```rust
   pub fn prompt_test_timeout() -> Result<u32, CliError>
   ```
   - Read from stdin
   - Validate input is a positive integer
   - Default to 120 if empty input

4. Define `CliError` enum using thiserror

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- `rust-bucket --help` shows usage
- `rust-bucket apply --help` shows apply options
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for CLI requirements
- clap documentation: https://docs.rs/clap/latest/clap/

title: Implement template embedding module
body:
## Goal
Implement the template embedding module using rust-embed.

## Tasks
1. Implement `src/templates.rs` with rust-embed

2. Define the embedded templates:
   ```rust
   #[derive(RustEmbed)]
   #[folder = "templates/"]
   pub struct Templates;
   ```

3. Implement template extraction to temp directory:
   ```rust
   pub fn extract_to_temp() -> Result<PathBuf, TemplateError>
   ```
   - Create a temp directory using `tempfile`
   - Extract all embedded files to the temp directory
   - Return the temp directory path

4. Implement listing of managed files:
   ```rust
   pub fn managed_files() -> Vec<&'static str>
   ```
   - Return list of all files that rust-bucket manages
   - This is used for conflict detection

5. Define `TemplateError` enum using thiserror

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Unit test that extraction creates expected files
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for template embedding requirements
- rust-embed documentation: https://docs.rs/rust-embed/latest/rust_embed/
- tempfile documentation: https://docs.rs/tempfile/latest/tempfile/

title: Implement generator module with cargo-generate
body:
## Goal
Implement the generator module using cargo-generate as a library.

## Tasks
1. Add cargo-generate as a dependency in Cargo.toml:
   ```toml
   cargo-generate = "0.22"
   ```

2. Implement `src/generator.rs`:

3. Create a function to render templates:
   ```rust
   pub fn render(
       template_dir: &Path,
       output_dir: &Path,
       config: &Config,
       overwrite: bool,
   ) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, GeneratorError>
   ```
   - Use cargo-generate programmatically
   - Pass config values as template variables:
     - rust_bucket_version
     - test_timeout
   - Return list of generated files

4. Handle overwrite modes:
   - If overwrite=false, fail if any target file exists
   - If overwrite=true, replace existing files

5. Define `GeneratorError` enum using thiserror:
   - TemplateError
   - IoError
   - ConflictError(Vec<PathBuf>) - list of conflicting files

6. If cargo-generate library API is difficult:
   - Document the issue
   - Consider falling back to direct liquid template rendering
   - Create a follow-up bead if needed

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Can render templates with variable substitution
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- Read ARCHITECTURE.md for generator requirements
- cargo-generate source: https://github.com/cargo-generate/cargo-generate
- If blocked, create bead to analyze cargo-generate API

title: Implement managed files registry
body:
## Goal
Implement the managed files registry that tracks which files rust-bucket controls.

## Tasks
1. Add to `src/generator.rs` or create `src/managed.rs`:

2. Define the complete list of managed files:
   ```rust
   pub const MANAGED_FILES: &[&str] = &[
       "AGENTS.md",
       "STYLE_GUIDE.md",
       "WORKFLOW.md",
       "TESTING.md",
       ".config/nextest.toml",
       "deny.toml",
       "rustfmt.toml",
       ".devcontainer/Dockerfile",
       ".devcontainer/devcontainer.json",
       ".beads/config.yaml",
   ];
   ```

3. Implement conflict detection:
   ```rust
   pub fn check_conflicts(target_dir: &Path) -> Vec<PathBuf>
   ```
   - Check which managed files already exist
   - Return list of existing files (conflicts)

4. Implement file existence check:
   ```rust
   pub fn has_rust_bucket_toml(target_dir: &Path) -> bool
   ```
   - Used to detect init vs update mode

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Conflict detection works correctly
- Unit tests for conflict detection

## References
- README.md for list of managed files
- ARCHITECTURE.md for overwrite policies

title: Implement verification module
body:
## Goal
Implement the verification module that runs cargo checks after generation.

## Tasks
1. Implement `src/verify.rs`:

2. Define verification steps:
   ```rust
   pub enum VerifyStep {
       Format,
       Clippy,
       Test,
   }
   ```

3. Implement running verification:
   ```rust
   pub fn run_all(target_dir: &Path) -> Result<VerifyReport, VerifyError>
   ```
   - Run `cargo fmt --check`
   - Run `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
   - Run `cargo nextest run`
   - Collect results for each step

4. Define `VerifyReport` struct:
   ```rust
   pub struct VerifyReport {
       pub format: StepResult,
       pub clippy: StepResult,
       pub test: StepResult,
   }
   
   pub enum StepResult {
       Pass,
       Fail(String), // error message
       Skip(String), // reason skipped
   }
   ```

5. Handle cases where commands aren't available:
   - If cargo-nextest not installed, report skip
   - If no tests exist, report pass

6. Define `VerifyError` enum using thiserror

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Can run verification commands and report results
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- TESTING.md for verification requirements
- AGENTS.md for Definition of Done

title: Implement init flow
body:
## Goal
Implement the first-time `rust-bucket apply` flow.

## Tasks
1. Implement in `src/lib.rs` or `src/apply.rs`:

2. Create the init flow function:
   ```rust
   pub fn apply_init(
       target_dir: &Path,
       force: bool,
   ) -> Result<ApplyResult, ApplyError>
   ```

3. Implement the flow per ARCHITECTURE.md:
   a. Assert Cargo.toml exists (this is a Rust crate)
   b. Assert .git/ exists (git init was done)
   c. Check for conflicts:
      - If conflicts exist and !force, fail with list of conflicts
      - If conflicts exist and force, warn and continue
   d. Prompt for choices (test_timeout)
   e. Create Config with choices
   f. Write rust-bucket.toml
   g. Extract templates to temp dir
   h. Render templates to target dir (overwrite=force)
   i. Run verification
   j. Return result

4. Define `ApplyResult` struct:
   ```rust
   pub struct ApplyResult {
       pub files_generated: Vec<PathBuf>,
       pub verification: VerifyReport,
   }
   ```

5. Define `ApplyError` enum:
   - NotRustCrate
   - NotGitRepo
   - ConflictingFiles(Vec<PathBuf>)
   - ConfigError
   - GeneratorError
   - VerifyError

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Init flow works end-to-end
- Proper error messages for each failure mode
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- ARCHITECTURE.md apply first time flow
- WORKFLOW.md for verification requirements

title: Implement update flow
body:
## Goal
Implement the subsequent `rust-bucket apply` flow (update mode).

## Tasks
1. Add to `src/lib.rs` or `src/apply.rs`:

2. Create the update flow function:
   ```rust
   pub fn apply_update(
       target_dir: &Path,
   ) -> Result<ApplyResult, ApplyError>
   ```

3. Implement the flow per ARCHITECTURE.md:
   a. Load rust-bucket.toml
   b. Validate config (check version compatibility if needed)
   c. Pre-populate choices from config
   d. Prompt for any NEW choices not in config (none in v1)
   e. Update config with any new values
   f. Write updated rust-bucket.toml
   g. Extract templates to temp dir
   h. Render templates to target dir (overwrite=true)
   i. Run verification
   j. Return result

4. Ensure version stamp is updated in all files

## Success Criteria
- `cargo check` passes
- `cargo clippy` passes
- Update flow works end-to-end
- Existing config values are preserved
- All managed files are regenerated
- No unwrap() or expect() in production code

## References
- ARCHITECTURE.md apply subsequent times flow

title: Wire modules together and complete main
Add integration tests
Research cargo-generate library API
body:
## Goal
If generator module (bd-c001) encounters difficulties with cargo-generate as a library, research and document the API.

## Trigger
Only execute this bead if bd-c001 fails or escalates due to cargo-generate issues.

## Tasks
1. Clone or read cargo-generate source code:
   - https://github.com/cargo-generate/cargo-generate

2. Analyze the library API:
   - Find the main entry points for programmatic use
   - Document required structs and functions
   - Identify how to:
     - Provide template directory
     - Pass template variables
     - Control overwrite behavior
     - Handle errors

3. Create `docs/CARGO_GENERATE_API.md` with:
   - Summary of findings
   - Code examples for our use case
   - Any limitations or workarounds
   - Alternative approaches if library use is impractical

4. If cargo-generate library use is impractical:
   - Document why
   - Propose using `liquid` crate directly
   - Update ARCHITECTURE.md with new approach

## Success Criteria
- Documentation created if bead is triggered
- Clear recommendation for how to proceed

## References
- cargo-generate source code
- liquid crate: https://docs.rs/liquid/latest/liquid/
